title: olea cassinifolia
intro: Olea cassinifolia, also known as the stink wood tree, is a species of evergreen tree native to the island of Madagascar. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Olea cassinifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Olea cassinifolia can be made into a tincture, infusion, or salve, and are often used topically to treat skin conditions and minor injuries.
